query:
Research the company "botanictonics".

Find the following company-level data: (1) Most recent quarterly or annual financial results, including any P&L gaps or shortfalls versus guidance; (2) Production growth rates and capacity utilization metrics; (3) Recent CEO changes, departures, or new leadership announcements; (4) Revised, pulled, or lowered guidance for upcoming fiscal year or quarters; (5) Portfolio structure (multiple brands, product lines, or platform launches); (6) Recent cost-cutting programs, restructuring, or operational efficiency initiatives; (7) Distribution channels, expansion plans, or channel strategy changes; (8) Pricing strategy changes or margin pressure signals; (9) Industry-specific growth drivers (manufacturing, media/advertising spend, promotional activity, demand forecasting challenges); (10) Scale/revenue size and comparison to Fortune 500 or $10B+ peer companies; (11) Any public disclosures about cross-functional alignment or siloed optimization challenges.

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
**Executive Summary**

Botanic Tonics has experienced rapid retail expansion and operational scaling over the past three years, claiming to have surpassed $250 million in annual sales profitability. The company has aggressively expanded its manufacturing footprint and achieved significant distribution milestones, including surpassing legacy brands like 5-hour Energy in key convenience channels. However, the last six months (January–June 2026) have been defined by a complex regulatory and legal environment. While the company successfully saw a major FDA case dismissed in January 2026, it is currently battling new state-level bans in Utah and managing the financial fallout of multiple class action settlements totaling over $16 million in claims. 

**Detailed Analysis**

**Financial Performance and Scale**
*   **Revenue and Growth:** In March 2025, CEO Cameron Korehbandi stated the company had surpassed $250 million in annual sales profitability during its third year of operation [Botanic Tonics, Maker of Feel Free, Recognized on Bain & Company's 2025 Insurgent Brands List | BevNET.com](https://www.bevnet.com/pr/2025/03/05/botanic-tonics-maker-of-feel-free-recognized-on-bain--companys-2025-insurgent-brands-list). However, third-party estimates from Growjo place the company's annual revenue significantly lower at $68.6 million [Botanic Tonics: Revenue, Competitors, Alternatives](https://growjo.com/company/Botanic_Tonics). The company has maintained at least 10% growth over the last two years and reports growing more than 10 times its category's average growth rate over the past five years [Botanic Tonics | LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/botanictonics).
*   **Capital and Investments:** In January 2025, Botanic Tonics secured a $25 million internal investment aimed at securing its kava supply chain and supporting testing efforts [Feelin’ Free: Botanic Tonics Adds Capital, Invests In Supply Chain - BevNET.com](https://www.bevnet.com/news/2025/feelin-free-botanic-tonics-adds-capital-invests-in-supply-chain/). 
*   **Litigation Liabilities:** The company has faced significant legal costs, agreeing to pay $8.75 million to settle a class action lawsuit (*In re Botanic Tonics Litigation*) regarding allegations that it failed to disclose the addictive nature of kratom in its products [Botanic Tonics Class Action Settlement: How To Claim $8.75M - Sparrow Blog](https://usesparrow.com/blog/botanic-tonics-class-action-settlement/). As of June 2025, the company faced four major class action settlements with total claims exceeding $16 million [Botanic Tonics - Products, Competitors, Financials, Employees, Headquarters Locations](https://www.cbinsights.com/company/botanic-tonics). 

**Production, Capacity, and Operational Efficiency**
*   **Manufacturing Footprint:** Botanic Tonics operates a vertically integrated manufacturing, fulfillment, and distribution facility in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma [Feel free: Driving Growth and Excellence in Oklahoma – Botanic Tonics](https://botanictonics.com/blogs/botanic-secrets/botanic-tonics-driving-growth-and-excellence-in-oklahoma). Reports on the facility's expansion vary, noting growth from an initial 30,000 square feet to either 93,250 square feet [Feel free: Driving Growth and Excellence in Oklahoma – Botanic Tonics](https://botanictonics.com/blogs/botanic-secrets/botanic-tonics-driving-growth-and-excellence-in-oklahoma) or 122,000 square feet [Feelin’ Free: Botanic Tonics Adds Capital, Invests In Supply Chain - BevNET.com](https://www.bevnet.com/news/2025/feelin-free-botanic-tonics-adds-capital-invests-in-supply-chain/).
*   **Capacity Utilization & Efficiency:** The company doubled its bottle production in the year leading up to early 2024 and planned to install an additional packaging line in Q3 2024 [Feel free: Driving Growth and Excellence in Oklahoma – Botanic Tonics](https://botanictonics.com/blogs/botanic-secrets/botanic-tonics-driving-growth-and-excellence-in-oklahoma). Operational efficiency initiatives successfully increased Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) from 50% to between 80% and 85% within a single year [Feel free: Driving Growth and Excellence in Oklahoma – Botanic Tonics](https://botanictonics.com/blogs/botanic-secrets/botanic-tonics-driving-growth-and-excellence-in-oklahoma). 
*   **Quality Restructuring:** To streamline testing, the company relocated its Research & Development Quality lab from California to Tulsa, Oklahoma. In December 2024, the Broken Arrow facility received a 97% performance rating in an Intertek audit [Botanic Tonics' Manufacturing Facility Receives 97% Performance Rating](https://botanictonics.com/blogs/press-releases/botanic-tonics-manufacturing-facility-receives-97-performance-rating-from-intertek).
*   **Supply Chain:** The company exclusively produces its own kava extracts but is currently evaluating commercializing its extraction operations for other brands [Feelin’ Free: Botanic Tonics Adds Capital, Invests In Supply Chain - BevNET.com](https://www.bevnet.com/news/2025/feelin-free-botanic-tonics-adds-capital-invests-in-supply-chain/).

**Distribution Channels and Portfolio Strategy**
*   **Channel Expansion:** Botanic Tonics has aggressively penetrated the convenience store channel. The company recently announced its first-ever corporate convenience store partnership with Circle K, placing its products in over 600 stores across Arizona and Nevada [Botanic Tonics | LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/botanictonics). 
*   **Market Share:** By late 2025, the "feel free" product surpassed 5-hour Energy in sales volume within its first major national convenience store chain partner [Botanic Tonics' "feel free" Surpasses 5-hour Energy in Major National Chain in Just Four Months](https://botanictonics.com/blogs/press-releases/botanic-tonics-feel-free-surpasses-5-hour-energy-in-major-national-chain-in-just-four-months). NIQ data from late 2025 ranked the brand in the top 4 energy and supplement brands at two major U.S. convenience chains [Botanic Tonics' feel free Soars Into Energy Category Elite, Claiming Top 4 Position Alongside Industry Giants](https://botanictonics.com/blogs/press-releases/botanic-tonics-feel-free-soars-into-energy-category-elite-claiming-top-4-position-alongside-industry-giants).
*   **Portfolio Milestones:** As of April 29, 2026, the company reported selling over 129 million servings of its products [Botanic Tonics Sets New Standard for Consumer Education with Industry-Leading Label Update | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/press-releases/botanic-tonics-consumer-education-label-update-2026-04-29/). On the same date, the company unveiled an updated label for its 'feel free CLASSIC' tonic to enhance transparency regarding ingredients and potential risks [Botanic Tonics Sets New Standard for Consumer Education with Industry-Leading Label Update | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/press-releases/botanic-tonics-consumer-education-label-update-2026-04-29/).
*   **Industry Recognition:** In March 2026, Botanic Tonics was named to Bain & Company’s Insurgent Brands list for the third consecutive year [Botanic Tonics, Maker of feel free, Named to Bain & Company’s 2026 Insurgent Brands List for Third Consecutive Year | BevNET.com](https://www.bevnet.com/pr/2026/03/04/botanic-tonics-maker-of-feel-free-named-to-bain--companys-2026-insurgent-brands-list-for-third-consecutive-year).

**Recent Regulatory and Political Activity (Last 6 Months)**
*   **Federal Wins:** On January 6, 2026, the U.S. government dismissed an FDA case (*U.S. v. 250,000 filled bottles of liquid product et al*) against the company in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Oklahoma [Government Dismisses FDA Case Against Botanic Tonics, Maker of feel free, Reflecting Regulatory Evolution on Natural Kratom Leaf Products | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/press-releases/government-dismisses-fda-case-botanic-tonics-kratom-leaf-2026-01-06/).
*   **State-Level Headwinds:** Due to new kratom regulations in Utah, Botanic Tonics is required to remove its kratom products from 321 retail locations in the state by May 6, 2026. In response, the company filed a federal lawsuit against Utah officials on March 31, 2026, seeking to declare the regulations unconstitutional [Makers of ‘feel free’ tonics sue Utah officials over new kratom regulations • Utah News Dispatch](https://utahnewsdispatch.com/2026/04/07/maker-of-feel-free-tonics-sues-utah-kratom-regulations/).
*   **Political Spending:** While the company reported $0 in federal lobbying expenditures for 2025 [Botanic Tonics Lobbying Profile • OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/federal-lobbying/clients/summary?id=D000107667), it made a $500,000 donation to the MAHA PAC in March 2026, marking the PAC's largest donation in the preceding year [The groups backing RFK Jr. are running low on cash - POLITICO](https://www.politico.com/news/2026/04/24/rfk-maha-lyons-pac-midterms-00889913).

**Gaps and Limitations**
Based on the provided research findings, the following requested data points could not be verified:
*   **(1) P&L gaps or shortfalls versus guidance:** No specific quarterly P&L statements, gross margin percentages, or earnings misses were available.
*   **(3) Recent CEO changes or departures:** Cameron Korehbandi was identified as CEO as of March 2025, but no recent leadership changes or departures were noted.
*   **(4) Revised, pulled, or lowered guidance:** No forward-looking financial guidance or revisions were disclosed.
*   **(8) Pricing strategy changes or margin pressure signals:** No data was provided regarding unit pricing, discounting strategies, or explicit margin pressures (beyond the existence of litigation costs).
*   **(10) Comparison to Fortune 500 or $10B+ peers:** The findings do not benchmark Botanic Tonics against Fortune 500 or $10B+ companies.
*   **(11) Cross-functional alignment or siloed optimization:** No internal corporate disclosures regarding cross-functional team alignment or silo challenges were present in the data.
